prompt: Create an ultra-realistic vertical editorial portrait photograph of a young creative artist woman seated casually on a white sculptural modern chair inside a giant empty indoor swimming pool drained of water and transformed into a raw artistic workspace. The woman is centered in the foreground, sitting with relaxed confidence, legs crossed, one arm draped over the chair back and the other resting near her lap. She has long wavy brown hair, natural makeup, and wears a loose dark charcoal button-up shirt with rolled sleeves, wide flowing white trousers, and white slide sandals. The environment is a vast tiled pool interior with high concrete walls, a curved glass-and-steel ceiling, large upper windows, and dramatic natural daylight casting geometric reflections and shadows across the floor. The back wall has a huge handwritten black slogan reading Everything Starts With An Idea... Surround the space with an organized creative-process mess: wall-mounted mood boards, sketches, taped photos, sticky notes, handwritten process notes, black-and-white prints, loose papers, open notebooks, cables, camera gear, small projectors, paint buckets, mirrored glass panels, transparent acrylic boards with diagrams, and scattered art tools. Include exactly 6 prominent floor objects in the foreground: 1 open sketchbook labeled with repeated “Creative Process” notes and yellow highlight, 1 black camera with lens, 1 coil of black cables, 1 mirrored glass panel, 1 paint bucket, and 1 loose printed concept sheet. Use a high-end magazine editorial style, photorealistic skin and fabric textures, wide-angle environmental portrait composition, shallow but readable depth of field, cinematic soft daylight, muted neutral palette, subtle teal-gray shadows, realistic perspective, and detailed clutter without looking chaotic. No watermark, no extra people, no distorted anatomy, no illegible gibberish except small background notes, and keep the subject’s face natural and unobstructed.
